commit.message:
fix: tray version null — copy data before dispatch_async

The Go Init() calls C.CString then defer C.free, but initTray uses
dispatch_async which runs the block later on the main thread. By the
time the block executes, the C string is already freed (use-after-free).

Fix: copy version string and icon data to ObjC objects before
dispatch_async, so the block captures retained objects instead of
raw pointers to freed memory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix bone rendering: sync outputs head position, add explicit joint anchors

Two fixes for visual bone stretching:

1. Sync was writing body midpoint position as bone_world translation,
   but skinning IBMs expect bone HEAD position. Now stores a per-bone
   body-local head_offset during creation and applies it in sync to
   recover the correct head position.

2. Added explicit anchor_a/anchor_b fields to bone_joint_desc_t so
   joint anchor positions can be exported from Blender rather than
   assumed from bone hierarchy. The exporter writes child bone HEAD
   as the anchor point for both parent and child bodies.

Files changed:
- bone_joint_desc.h: Added anchor_a[3], anchor_b[3], has_anchors
- phys_anim_entity.h: Added head_offsets array to entity struct
- phys_anim_entity_create.c: Store head offsets, use exported anchors
- phys_anim_entity_sync.c: Offset body midpoint → bone head in sync
- fskel_load.c: Parse anchor_a/anchor_b from joint_desc
- fskel_write.c: Write anchor data when has_anchors is set
- export_fskel.py: Export joint anchor positions

Fix md_parse_sequence_points document and rolling value bugs (#68)

Two bugs in sequence point parsing:

1. When the MethodDebugInformation row has a non-null Document
   column (the common case for single-document methods), the parser
   read the cursor into a local variable but never copied it to
   sequence_points->document. The field was left at an invalid row-0
   default, causing callers to fail when resolving the document name.

2. The rolling_il_offset, rolling_start_line, and rolling_start_column
   fields were not accumulated. The Portable PDB spec delta-encodes
   these values (absolute for the first record, signed deltas for
   subsequent records), but the parser stored raw deltas instead of
   running sums. This caused incorrect source locations for any
   sequence point after the first.

Fix both by:
  - Copying the Document column cursor directly when non-null.
  - Maintaining running accumulators for IL offset, start line,
    and start column across records.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
network: get_connected_peers() only returns post-handshake peers with live writers

Previously, get_connected_peers() returned every entry in the connections map,
including peers in the Connecting state (mark_connecting() was called but TCP
handshake hadn't completed or had already timed out). This caused the AI peer
selector and sync logic to receive and attempt to use peers that weren't
actually reachable, e.g. 34.170.12.145 appearing as a sync candidate despite
the connection timing out.

Fix: filter the connections map to only IPs that have an active (non-closed)
writer channel in peer_writers. Writers are only registered after a successful
handshake (register_peer / register_peer_shared), so this is a reliable
post-handshake liveness check. Applied the same filter to peer_count().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix compliance softening for tiered substeps

Compliance factor (α·inv_dt²) was using max_substeps inv_dt=240 for all
bodies, but T0/T1 bodies with fewer substeps have an effective inv_dt of
120. This caused 4× over-softening for ghost-to-ghost joints (compliance
factor 28.8 vs intended 7.2).

Now computes per-body effective inv_dt from tier_substep_counts/tick_dt.
Also restored ERP to 0.4 positional / 0.05 angular (was 0.15/0.02).

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add AsyncSeq.removeAt, updateAt, insertAt — 3 new Seq-mirroring combinators (#268)

* Add AsyncSeq.removeAt, updateAt, insertAt — mirror Seq combinators

These three F# 6.0 Seq-mirroring combinators enable in-place mutation-style
operations on async sequences:

- removeAt: skip the element at the given index
- updateAt: replace the element at the given index with a new value
- insertAt: insert a value before the element at the given index
           (or append when index == length)

All three raise ArgumentException for negative indices. insertAt also
raises ArgumentException when index exceeds the sequence length.

14 new tests added; 301/301 pass.

* Add JSON-LD structured data support to HtmlProvider

Adds native JSON-LD support to HtmlProvider: when an HTML document contains
<script type="application/ld+json"> blocks, the provider now generates a
typed .JsonLd container (e.g. doc.JsonLd.Article) with one strongly-typed
string property per top-level scalar field discovered in the sample.

This mirrors the .Schemas container added for HTML microdata and is especially
useful for Wikipedia pages (and many modern websites) which embed schema.org
Article/WebPage/Person JSON-LD in the <head> element for SEO purposes.

- Add JsonLdGroup case to HtmlObjectDescription
